sound_type: thin high whistle -- reedy, high-pitched whistle or trill near nest sites. Close-range courtship. Quiet after loud Scolopacidae. E=3
ecosystem: Rocky coastlines, jetties, under wharves and docks. Sea cliffs. Nearshore waters for feeding. Urban waterfronts.
season: Summer breeder along PNW coast. Present year-round in some areas. Most visible spring through fall.

coordinates.description: Pigeon Guillemot nesting under docks and on rocky shoreline. Elliott Bay, Seattle waterfront. Urban seabird.

taxonomy_note.description: FIRST ALCIDAE. The auk family enters the engine. Wing-propelled divers. After six Scolopacidae (168-173). Family transition: shore to sea. New genus: Cepphus (guillemots).
taxonomy_note.name_etymology: Cepphus from Greek 'kepphos' (a seabird). columba from Latin 'columba' (pigeon/dove) -- Pigeon Guillemot named for pigeon-like appearance. Guillemot from French 'Guillaume' (William) -- a personal name applied to the bird.
taxonomy_note.genus_note: Cepphus -- guillemots. 3 species. Nearshore alcids. Rocky coast nesters. Bright red or orange feet.
taxonomy_note.order_note: Charadriiformes. First Alcidae after six Scolopacidae (168-173). Fundamental family transition.
taxonomy_note.family_position: First Alcidae. Opens the auk era. Wing-propelled diving. Urban nesting. Red feet.

song_metrics.song_duration_s: Each call 0.5-2 seconds. Short trills or sustained thin whistles.
song_metrics.note_structure: Thin, high-pitched, reedy whistle: 'peeeeeer' or rapid trill 'ti-ti-ti-ti-ti.' Close-range. Intimate.
song_metrics.note_types: thin whistle (primary -- courtship/nest-site), rapid chattering 'ki-ki-ki-ki' (aggression), soft clucking (courtship display).
song_metrics.phrase_structure: Single thin whistles or short trills. Not elaborate sequences. Close-range communication.
song_metrics.timbre: Thin, reedy, high-pitched. Does not carry far. Intimate quality. Acoustic contrast to loud Scolopacidae.
song_metrics.amplitude: E=3. Quiet. Audible at close range only. Rocky shoreline intimacy.
song_metrics.tempo: Calls given intermittently near nest sites. Not rapid or insistent.
song_metrics.context: Courtship, nest-site interaction, mate communication. Rocky shoreline and under docks.
song_metrics.key_property: THIN HIGH WHISTLE -- quiet, intimate, nest-site. FIRST ALCIDAE. Bright red feet. Wing-propelled diving. Urban nesting. E=3. v1.49.314.

plumage.overall: Breeding: entirely dark (black-brown) with large white wing patch and bright red-orange feet. Non-breeding: paler, mottled gray-white above, white below, white wing patch retained.
plumage.diagnostic_mark: Bright red-orange feet. White wing patch. Dark plumage (breeding). Compact build.
plumage.seasonal_variation: Breeding: dark with white patch, bright red feet. Non-breeding: mottled, duller feet.
plumage.flight_pattern: Fast, buzzy, low over water. Short stiff wings beat rapidly. White wing patch visible.

physical_description.overall: Length: 30-35 cm. Weight: 450-550g. Bill: short, dark, pointed. Legs and feet: bright red-orange (breeding). Compact, stocky build.
physical_description.diagnostic_mark: Bright red feet. White wing patch. Dark compact body. Short bill.
physical_description.field_id: Compact dark seabird on rocky shoreline or near docks. Bright red feet diagnostic. White wing patch. Listen for thin high whistle near nest sites. Fast buzzy flight low over water.

nodes.title: Thin High Whistle -- Rocky Shoreline Intimacy
nodes.description: Thin, high-pitched, reedy whistle near nest sites. Close-range courtship. E=3. Quiet after loud Scolopacidae. The sound of the rocky shoreline, not the open flat.

nodes.title: First Alcidae -- Auk Family Enters
nodes.description: The engine transitions from Scolopacidae (168-173, shorebirds) to Alcidae (auks, seabirds). Shore to sea. Walking to diving. Wing-propelled underwater flight.

nodes.title: Wing-Propelled Diving -- Flying Underwater
nodes.description: Dual-use wings for air and water. Dive 10-45m (up to 60+). Short stiff wings: fast buzzy aerial flight + efficient underwater propulsion. Northern Hemisphere penguins.

nodes.title: Urban Nesting -- Under Docks
nodes.description: Nests in crevices under wharves, piers, ferry terminals. Elliott Bay waterfront. Breeds within meters of human activity. Bright red feet in courtship. An urban seabird.

cross_references.relationship: FAMILY TRANSITION! Last Scolopacidae (173) to first Alcidae (174). Shore to sea. Loud alarm to thin whistle. Walking to diving. Fundamental shift.
HABITAT CONTRAST! Dunlin: tidal flat, massive flocks, E=3. Guillemot: rocky coast, small colonies, E=3. Same energy, utterly different worlds.
ALCIDAE ERA! First (174) and second (175) Alcidae. Guillemot: nearshore, visible, urban. Auklet: offshore, nocturnal colony. Family expanding.
ALCIDAE ERA! Guillemot (174) to Puffin (176). Same family. Both nest in rocky crevices. Puffin: iconic species, conservation crisis.
